LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING RECEIVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING A RECEIVER GRANTED. UPON DEFAULT, CONTRACT GIVES THE RECEIVER THE POWER TO TAKE POSSESSION OF ALL PROPERTY, WITH THE POWER TO PROTECT AND PRESERVE, TO OPERATE PRECEDING A FORECLOSURE OR SALE AND COLLECT RENTS. DEFENDANT DEFAULTED AND DOES NOT PRESENTLY HAVE SUFFICIENT FUNDS TO RESUME DEVELOPMENT. COMPLETION OF THE BUILDING APPEARS PROPER TO PROTECT AND PRESERVE PLAINTIFF'S SECURITY INTEREST IN THE BUILDING. PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED. LOANS TOTAL OVER $8.7 MILLION. DEFENDANT'S LOANS ARE SECURED BY PROPERTY THAT IS ONLY 50 % DEVELOPED. IF PROPERTY REMAINS IDLE, VALUE WILL DECREASE IF IT REMAINS IN ITS CURRENT CONDITION. IMMINENT IRREPARABLE HARM DEMONSTRATED BY INCREASED RISK OF VANDALISM, WEATHER DAMAGE AND DECREASE IN VALUE DUE TO STALLED CONSTRUCTION. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AND THE HARM TO PLAINTIFF OUTWEIGHS THE HARM TO DEFENDANT. THE COURT SETS THE AMOUNT FOR THE RECEIVER'S BOND AT 10,000.00 AND THE AMOUNT OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BOND AT $500,000.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANTS BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY, CHRISTOPHER GREENE, AND, MARK MCDONALD DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT. HAMROL PLACES A CLEARLY ERRONEOUS INTERPRETATION ON THE WRITTEN TERMS OF THE CONSTRUCTION LOAN AGREEMENT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BREACH OF ORAL CONTRACT REGARDING GREENE. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO REMAINDER. HAMROL'S FRAUD ALLEGATIONS DO NOT DIRECTLY VARY WITH THE TERMS OF THE WRITTEN CONTRACT AND THEREFORE, THE FRAUD EXCEPTION TO THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE CAN APPLY. OVERRULED AS TO MONEY HAD AND RECEIVED. PARAGRAPH 13 ALLEGES TRANSFER OF MONEY TO BOREL. CROSS-COMPLAINANT SHALL FILE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT TO REDRAFT BREACH OF ORAL CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION TO CONFORM WITH THE COURT'S RULING. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MAY NOT ADD ANY ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT COURT'S APPROVAL. THE COURT WILL GRANT LEAVE FOR PLAINTIFF TO ADD NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS TO RE-DRAFT ITS CROSS COMPLAINT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Receiver And Preliminary Injuction
Off Calendar Jan-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY Motion for Order Appointing Receiver and Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Todd C. Toral, Esq. & Andrew R. Neilson, Esq of Nixon Peabody LLP (415) 984-8268, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Wallace C. Dootlitte, Esq. of Law Office of Wallace C. Doolittle (510) 888-0603 appearing for all defendants. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for order appointing a receiver granted. Upon default, contract gives the receiver the power to take possession of all property, with the power to protect and preserve, to operate preceding a foreclosure or sale and collect rents. Defendant defaulted and does not presently have sufficient funds to resume development. Completion of the building appears proper to protect and preserve plaintiff's security interest in the building. Preliminary Injunction granted. Loans total over $8.7 million. Defendant's loans are secured by property that is only 50 % developed. If property remains idle, value will decrease if it remains in its current condition. Imminent irreparable harm demonstrated by increased risk of vandalism, weather damage and decrease in value due to stalled construction. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits and the harm to plaintiff outweighs the harm to defendant. The Court sets the amount for the Receiver's Bond at 10,000.00 and the amount of the Preliminary Injunction Bond at $500,000.00. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
